assertions.description: Agent detects or asks about the user's operating system before suggesting any package install commands (apt/brew/scoop)
Agent creates a backup of existing vim/nvim configuration before making any modifications
Generated init.vim uses has('nvim') guards to isolate Neovim-only code (Lua, LSP) from shared Vimscript settings
Agent creates a Neovim symlink (~/.config/nvim) pointing to the shared ~/.vim/ directory
Agent selects the correct package manager based on detected platform (Scoop for Windows, Homebrew for macOS, apt for Ubuntu)
Agent provides verification commands that confirm both vim and nvim load correctly after deployment
Output includes a summary of every action taken (packages installed, files created, backups made)
Agent handles 'no editor installed' case by recommending Neovim installation with an explanation of why Neovim is preferred over plain Vim
Agent does not suggest destructive commands (rm -rf, force-overwrite) on existing config without first creating a backup or asking for confirmation
init.vim template includes lazy.nvim bootstrap code so the plugin manager self-installs on first Neovim launch
